items.why: Students use magnetism as the test, but stainless steel (ferrous) can be non-magnetic. The defining property is iron content - 'ferrous' comes from the Latin for iron.

items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.q: What is FEA used for in product design?
items.correct: Simulating how a product will respond to forces, heat, or vibration before manufacture.
items.misconception: Final exam analysis of student designs.
items.random_wrong: Counting the elements in a chemical compound.
items.why: Students unfamiliar with the acronym guess at it. FEA = Finite Element Analysis: dividing a part into many small elements and simulating physical behaviour computationally - saves prototyping costs.
The answer examiners want is: Notes around a drawing. “A type of pencil” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Small quick sketches. “Photos of thumbs” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly how many thumbnails to aim for in early ideation?
items.why: The real figure is 20. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
